title: Corporate planning and implementation
summary: 9609 A Level — SMART objectives, planning cycle, resource allocation, and implementing strategy.
learningObjectives: Set SMART objectives from strategic goals.
Evaluate barriers to successful strategy implementation.

simpleExplanation.title: From plan to action
simpleExplanation.summary: Corporate planning turns strategy into measurable objectives, budgets, and timelines — then communicates and monitors delivery across the business.
steps: Set SMART objectives from strategic goals.
Monitor KPIs and adjust if off-track.

flashcards.back: Insufficient finance, resistant culture, poor communication, weak leadership.
Market share %, ROCE, customer satisfaction, unit cost — track progress.
Functional budgets implement corporate financial plan.
Workforce planning delivers people for strategic projects (2.1.2).
Backup plans if assumptions fail — reduces strategic risk.
Day-to-day ops diverge from strategy without monitoring.

sections.content: **Corporate planning and implementation** (6.2.2) completes **Topic 6**. A Level essays often stop at strategy choice — **top marks** require **SMART objectives**, **resources**, and **implementation risks**.

sections.question: Strategy: enter Germany within 18 months (market development). Propose two SMART objectives and one implementation barrier.
sections.solution:
**SMART 1:** Achieve **50 franchised stores** in Germany by **Q4 Year 2** (Specific, Measurable, Time-bound).

**SMART 2:** Reach **€20 m revenue** in Germany with **break-even** by **Month 20** (Measurable, Relevant to finance).

**Barrier:** **Cultural/localisation** — German consumers may differ from UK **promotion** (3.3.5) and **product** tastes; need **local market research** (3.2) and **German-speaking** management — risk of **slow rollout** if underestimated.
